items.title: Texas SNAP begins restricting sweetened drinks and candy starting April 1, 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says Texas begins restricting SNAP purchases of sweetened drinks and candy starting April 1, 2026. In FNS’s retailer notice, “sweetened drinks” are defined as non-alcoholic beverages made with water that contain 5 grams or more of added sugar (or any amount of artificial sweetener), with exclusions for milk/milk substitutes, beverages with more than 50% juice, certain low-sugar drinks sweetened with stevia/monk fruit, and medical-grade electrolyte drinks.

items.title: USDA: Florida SNAP restrictions on soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts take effect April 20, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists Florida’s target implementation date as April 20, 2026 for restrictions on soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts purchased with SNAP benefits. The same USDA page lists other April 2026 start dates in Texas (April 1) and Colorado (April 30).

items.title: USDA: Colorado SNAP soft drink restriction scheduled for April 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists Colorado’s target implementation date as April 30, 2026 for restrictions on soft drinks purchased with SNAP benefits. USDA’s list also shows April 2026 restriction start dates in Texas (April 1) and Florida (April 20).

items.title: CMS guidance: states transition certain Medicaid expansion adults to six-month renewals in 2027
items.summary: A CMS letter issued March 6, 2026 outlines how states may transition adult Medicaid expansion beneficiaries to six-month eligibility redeterminations in 2027. CMS describes two approaches: rescheduling renewals to begin no earlier than Jan. 1, 2027, or applying the six-month renewal requirement at an enrollee’s next scheduled renewal in 2027.

items.title: NACo summary: Medicaid six-month renewals begin Jan. 1, 2027; State Plan Amendment due March 31, 2027
items.summary: The National Association of Counties summarizes CMS guidance stating that, beginning Jan. 1, 2027, states must conduct eligibility redeterminations every six months for adults in Medicaid expansion coverage (and equivalent coverage under certain Section 1115 waivers). NACo also notes CMS says states must submit a State Plan Amendment by March 31, 2027 to confirm they will conduct six-month renewals.

items.title: IRS: Maximum Earned Income Tax Credit for 2026 rises to $8,231 for families with 3+ children
items.summary: In its annual inflation adjustments for tax year 2026, the IRS says the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit amount is $8,231 for eligible filers with three or more qualifying children (up from $8,046 for tax year 2025). The IRS noted these figures apply to tax year 2026 returns typically filed in 2027.

items.title: HUD guidance lists cost-saving options for Housing Choice Voucher agencies facing funding shortfalls
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2025-28 (summarized by NAHRO) lists steps public housing agencies can take to reduce Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) expenses and prevent voucher funding shortfalls. Examples include reviewing payment standards to stay within 90%–110% of Fair Market Rent, reducing or stopping issuance of turnover vouchers, revising subsidy standards, and requesting HUD approval for deeper actions like applying payment-standard decreases during a HAP contract term.

items.title: USDA: WIC state agencies must allow at least one non-fresh fruit/vegetable form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA Food and Nutrition Service says recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. USDA also says that as of November 2025, 95% of state agencies were already in compliance.

items.title: SSA: 2026 COLA is 2.8% for Social Security and SSI
items.summary: The Social Security Administration says Social Security and SSI benefits increase 2.8% in 2026. SSA says increased Social Security benefits are payable in January 2026 and increased SSI payments begin on December 31, 2025.

items.title: Workforce Pell proposed rule: public comments due April 8, 2026
items.summary: Higher Ed Dive reports the Department of Education issued a proposed rule to govern the new Workforce Pell Grant program, opening a 30-day public comment period. The article notes the proposal would expand Pell eligibility to certain short-term programs as short as eight weeks, with comments due April 8, 2026.

items.title: USDA posts updated list of SNAP food restriction waivers and target implementation dates
items.summary: USDA Food and Nutrition Service published an updated SNAP Food Restriction Waivers list on March 30, 2026, including April 2026 target start dates for restrictions in Texas (April 1), Florida (April 20), and Colorado (April 30). The USDA list specifies the restricted product categories by state (for example, Texas: sweetened drinks and candy).
